content.claim: Past administrative desegregation measures (enrollment rules, progression guarantees) temporarily reduced segregation in urban areas by the mid-2010s and lowered selection based on ethnicity and socioeconomic status within institutions, but these effects stagnated and did not fully reverse segregation trends.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Effect persistence after measurement period unknown; stagnation mechanisms not explained
Administrative interventions did not eliminate tracking structures, limiting test of structural versus non-structural remedies
Single policy line; no randomized or quasi-experimental comparison of alternative interventions
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
